nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich areas of lagoon and seaward reefs, from below the surge zone to at least 30 m depth; tends to shelter between corals, in crevices, and in shallow caves

diet.notes: Primarily algae-based; has been documented grazing on microalgae, marine algae, and blue-green algae growing on live rock and substrate. Frozen and dried foods containing marine and blue-green algae are generally accepted. Adequate algae availability tends to reduce risk of coral or clam nipping.

avoidWith: other blennies of the same genus

summary: A small, colorful herbivorous blenny from Pacific reefs, generally considered easy to keep and well-suited to reef aquariums; may occasionally nip at clam mantles or LPS corals if underfed.
careNotes: The Comical Blenny (also sold as the Latticed Blenny) is generally considered a good choice for beginner reef keepers given its hardy nature and peaceful temperament toward most tankmates. It tends to perch on rocks and rubble, using a mixed gravel or rubble substrate for shelter. A tight-fitting lid is strongly advised as Ecsenius blennies have been known to jump. The species has been known to show territorial aggression toward other blennies of the same genus, so keeping multiple Ecsenius species in the same aquarium is generally not advised unless the system is very large with ample territory. Reef safety is generally good but not absolute — isolated cases of nipping at clam mantles and fleshy LPS corals have been reported, particularly when the fish is not receiving sufficient algae in its diet. Ensuring a steady supply of algae-based foods tends to reduce this risk. Sources disagree slightly on temperature: the aquarium hobby commonly recommends 22–26°C (72–78°F) consistent with standard reef tank ranges, while FishBase wild habitat data indicates 27–29°C. A practical aquarium range of approximately 24–28°C is considered reasonable based on both sources. The species inhabits coral-rich lagoon and seaward reef environments in the Pacific, from Micronesia and the Gilbert Islands through Samoa, with reports from the Philippines and Wallis Island.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing behavior documented in the wild. Eggs are demersal and adhesive, attached to substrate via a filamentous adhesive pad. Larvae are planktonic. Captive breeding is not commonly reported in the aquarium hobby.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (5.0 cm TL matches LiveAquaria's 2-inch claim), native range, taxonomy, and habitat. Temperature disagreement noted: FishBase wild habitat 27.4–29.4°C vs. LiveAquaria aquarium recommendation 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); aquarium range of 24–28°C used as a practical average. FishBase confirmed Family Blenniidae, Order Blenniiformes, and Pacific distribution across Micronesia, Gilbert Islands, Rotuma, Samoa, Philippines, and Wallis Island.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; demersal adhesive eggs attached to substrate. Captive breeding is not commonly achieved in hobby aquariums.
